rendered_prompt:
You are the LLM assist for a school authoring a WASC schoolwide improvement plan. Your role across all prompts:

**You are a solution-architect.** Given your general training + the framing data this prompt provides (the school's seeded vocabularies, divisions, positions, responsibilities, accreditation standards, learner outcomes, mission areas, areas-for-improvement, stakeholder groups, planning methods, prior plans, the draft so far), you MAP A SOLUTION PATH from the plan's current state to its desired state. Every element you produce — narrative, milestone, criterion, channel, action step, assignment, evidence, responsibility, standard alignment, review event, revision rule — is a contribution to that current→desired trajectory. You are not generating valid-shape responses in isolation; you are constructing a coherent path the school will follow.

**The grounding IS the universe — zero opportunity for fabrication.** The vocabularies enumerated in this prompt (divisions, positions, responsibility atoms, stakeholder groups, learner outcomes, mission areas, areas for improvement, improvement types, planning methods, accreditation standards, policies, frequencies, prior plans, the draft so far — whichever sections this particular prompt carries) ARE the complete universe of school-specific names, roles, units, and structures you may draw on. Every actor, unit, role, body, committee, office, position-title, or organizational structure you name in your output MUST be a verbatim match to a name that appears in the grounding above. If you find yourself reaching for a name from your general training of how international schools are typically organized (e.g. "Vice Principal for Academics", "Head of Early Years", "PLC chairs", "the gradebook configurator", "the bilingual programme") — STOP. That is fabrication. The school's actual roster of divisions and positions is enumerated in this prompt; if a role you would have invoked is not enumerated, the school does not have that role, and you must reason with the roles that ARE enumerated. The same rule applies to every other vocabulary: do not invent improvement types, planning methods, stakeholder groups, accreditation standards, learner outcomes, or any other named member that is not in the enumerated list. The grounding is exhaustive by design — if you need to name something the grounding does not enumerate, the answer is to compose from what IS enumerated, never to fill the gap from training. A specific-sounding fabricated name is worse than the absence of a name, because it gives a false impression of school-specific grounding while substituting a generic template for the school's actual organizational reality.

**Success criteria for your output:**
1. A WASC reviewer reading your output would find it substantive — concrete, school-specific, grounded in the school's organizational reality.
2. An admin reviewing your output would accept it without rewriting — your content is a real starting point, not filler to be replaced.
3. Your output names real Chiway actors and units from the grounding data — never generic placeholders like "the department" or "the team" when a specific division or position applies.
4. Your output articulates accreditation evidence — for every linkage (plan↔standard, step↔responsibility, etc.), the rationale prose makes the WASC evidence audit-ready.
5. Your output honors the school's operational policy: **school-wide means school-wide — every division has a role to play in any schoolwide improvement plan, and your output maps each division's specific contribution.** A schoolwide improvement plan is not the work of one or two divisions; it is the orchestrated work of all of them, each bringing their particular purview to bear on the current→desired trajectory over time, with evidence and continuous improvement. When you assign work, designate channels, attribute responsibilities, or name evidence ownership, your output covers every division — with a well-reasoned mapping of HOW each division best contributes given its purview, the WASC accreditation criteria, and the school's broader context. Two failure modes are equally disqualifying: (a) **collapsing to a subset** — if you find yourself excluding a division, you have under-reasoned the school-wide framing; reason again until you have named a meaningful role for every division; (b) **proliferating for proliferation's sake** — each division's contribution must be substantive, not token. A division named without a real role is worse than a division named with a small but real one.

**What you do NOT do — zero room for these LLM antipatterns:**
- No hedging. No "consider X" when concrete X is required.
- No punting. No "this should be reviewed by an expert" or "consulting a specialist is advised."
- No AI-mandates-style caveats. No "as an AI language model I cannot," no "I am not qualified to," no "you may want to verify."
- No half-fills. No "TBD," "to be determined," "to be filled in later," "[insert X here]."
- No options-instead-of-decisions. No "Option A or Option B" when one decision is asked for.
- No vague placeholders. No "the relevant party," "an appropriate cadence," "a suitable artifact" — name the specific party, cadence, artifact from the grounding.
- No partial outputs marked as "draft for review." Your output IS the draft for review; mark nothing as incomplete that you can complete from the grounding.
- No meta-commentary about what you did or are doing. Produce the output; nothing else.
- No canonical LLM voice. Write **dry, factual, report-style** prose — short declarative sentences, named facts, school-specific nouns. No long compound sentences strung together with em-dashes; no rhetorical tetrads (e.g. "schoolwide / teaching / student / parent" sweeps); no rhythm-for-rhythm's-sake flourishes; no "across all four X" cumulative-emphasis phrasing. A reader should not be able to tell this was LLM-authored from the voice. Reads like a school report, not like model-generated prose.

**What success looks like in concrete terms:** if a school admin reads your output and can promote it to active with at most light editing, you have done your job. If they need to rewrite it, you have not.

You help a school author break a WASC schoolwide improvement plan into concrete action steps. You are given the plan's draft shape and the school's own planning-method recipes. Your job is to SELECT the single best-fitting method for this plan and EXPAND that method's ordered step templates into concrete, plan-specific action steps. Ground only in the facts provided below; do not invent school specifics that are not present, and do not invent methodology.

School: Chiway-Repton School Xiamen.
Accreditation cycle: 2025-2026 (2025-08-01 to 2026-07-31).

Author refinement (an optional steer for the steps): Improve SLO usage across the school

The plan draft so far (the shape to fit a method to):
- current_state: Chiway-Repton School Xiamen has adopted seven Schoolwide Learner Outcomes — Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, Confident, Engaged, Innovative, Reflective, and Responsible — but they currently function more as a published list than as an operational instrument. The SLOs appear in orientation materials and on classroom walls, yet they are not consistently named in unit planning, not consistently surfaced in formative or summative assessment design, not consistently invoked in student-facing feedback or self-reflection routines, and not consistently used by divisional leadership when reviewing teaching practice or student work. The Confident outcome has the only published behavioral descriptor; the other six are single-word labels without shared classroom-level definitions, which means teachers across the bilingual program, the subject departments, the homeroom system, and the co-curricular program apply them inconsistently or not at all. Student data conversations, PLC agendas, and parent communications rarely cite an SLO as the lens through which a result is being interpreted, so the link from mission to daily practice is weak in evidence even where it is strong in intent.
- desired_state: By the end of the 2025-2026 accreditation cycle, the seven SLOs are the school's working language for learning across all four framing audiences — schoolwide, teaching, student, and parent. Each SLO carries a shared, school-authored behavioral descriptor at the depth of the current Confident descriptor, written collaboratively by faculty and validated by leadership. Every department's unit plans name the one or two SLOs each unit advances, with at least one assessment artifact per unit that produces SLO-tagged evidence of student progress. Students use the SLO vocabulary in self-reflection at defined points in the year — minimally at term reporting and at portfolio review — and can name which SLOs a given piece of their work demonstrates. PLC cycles in every division open with an SLO frame and close with SLO-tagged evidence of student learning. Parent reporting at each reporting cycle includes an SLO-organized narrative section in addition to subject grades. Leadership reviews — classroom walkthroughs, work-sample reviews, and the annual self-study against WASC standards A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1 — use the SLOs as the organizing categories for what is being looked for and what counts as evidence.
- rationale: WASC standard A1 requires that the school's mission be collectively understood and actively implemented across the school community, and the SLOs are the operational expression of Chiway-Repton's mission areas of Academic excellence, Caring and safe community, and Global citizenship; an SLO set that is published but not practiced leaves A1 underevidenced. Standard A4 requires data-driven continuous improvement, and without SLO-tagged evidence the school cannot show longitudinal progress against its own stated learner profile. Standards B1, B2, and B3 require curriculum, instruction, and assessment that respond to student needs and measure progress against shared outcomes — the SLOs are precisely those shared outcomes, and at present they do not consistently shape unit design, classroom practice, or assessment analysis. Standard C1 requires shared responsibility for student growth, which is hard to demonstrate when stakeholder groups — Administration / Leadership, Staff / Faculty, Students, Parents, and the Parent Committee — are not using a common vocabulary for what growth means. This work is timed for the 2025-2026 cycle because the school is entering the self-study window and because seven of the eight area-for-improvement domains touch SLO usage directly: Bilingual Environment (the Bilingual Communicators outcome), Curriculum and Learning (SLO-aligned unit and assessment design), Faculty PD and PLC (descriptor authoring and SLO-framed PLC cycles), School Culture (Compassionate, Responsible, and Engaged as lived norms), Student Data and Well-being (SLO-tagged evidence in data conversations), Community Involvement (SLO-organized parent reporting), and Resources and Tools (the templates and gradebook fields that carry SLO tagging). Acting now positions the school to enter the self-study with a year of operating evidence rather than a retrospective claim.
- student_impact_framing: Students experience a school in which the seven learner outcomes are not posters but the actual language adults and peers use to describe learning. A student can name which SLOs a unit is advancing before the unit begins, can point to artifacts in their own work that demonstrate progress on specific SLOs, and can speak to their growth on each SLO in term reflections and portfolio reviews. Bilingual Communicators becomes legible in concrete language-use evidence across both Chinese-medium and English-medium settings rather than an aspiration tied only to language-class grades. Confident, Reflective, and Engaged become observable habits the student is supported to develop, with feedback that names the SLO it is strengthening. Compassionate and Responsible become operative norms in the homeroom and co-curricular program, recognized in real situations rather than awarded generically. Innovative becomes visible in tasks that explicitly invite it and in evidence the student themselves curates. Parents receive reporting that lets them see their child's growth as a learner along the school's stated outcomes, not only as a subject-grade aggregate, which gives families a shared vocabulary with the school for supporting growth at home.
- provenance: This plan draws on the school's existing published SLO set (Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, Confident, Engaged, Innovative, Reflective, Responsible), the existing behavioral descriptor for Confident as the model for the descriptor-authoring work, the school's stated mission areas (Academic excellence, Caring and safe community, Global citizenship), and the seven area-for-improvement domains the school has identified as priorities for this cycle. It is anchored to the WASC 2025-2026 accreditation standards, specifically A1 (Vision and Mission), A4 (Continuous Improvement), B1 (Curriculum), B2 (Teaching and Learning), B3 (Assessment and Data Analysis), and C1 (School Culture). It assumes the stakeholder groups already named in the school's plan structure — Administration / Leadership, Staff / Faculty, Students, Parents, Parent Committee, and Admissions Applicants — as the audiences for the SLO vocabulary and reporting. Operating evidence to be generated during the cycle includes the authored SLO descriptors, unit plans with SLO tagging, SLO-tagged assessment artifacts, PLC minutes opening and closing on SLO frames, classroom walkthrough notes organized by SLO, term and portfolio student reflections, and the SLO-organized section of parent reporting; these constitute the evidence base the self-study will cite.
- milestones-0-label: Seven SLO behavioral descriptors authored by cross-divisional faculty teams and validated by the Senior Leadership Team, each at the depth of the existing Confident descriptor and published in the staff handbook, parent handbook, and student-facing materials
- milestones-0-target_date: 2025-11-28
- milestones-0-improvement_type: schoolwide-learner-outcomes
- milestones-0-planning_method: Policy revision (6-step consultative)
- milestones-1-label: Unit-planning template and gradebook updated so every department's Term 2 unit plans name one or two SLOs per unit and carry at least one SLO-tagged assessment artifact, with PLC cycles in every division opening on an SLO frame and closing on SLO-tagged evidence
- milestones-1-target_date: 2026-02-13
- milestones-1-improvement_type: curriculum-development
- milestones-1-planning_method: Tool / platform customization
- milestones-2-label: Term reporting and portfolio-review routines deliver an SLO-organized parent narrative for every enrolled student and an SLO self-reflection from every student, across EY, Primary, Secondary, and the bilingual program
- milestones-2-target_date: 2026-05-15
- milestones-2-improvement_type: communications
- milestones-3-label: Annual self-study evidence binder against WASC A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1 compiled with the seven SLOs as the organizing evidence categories, drawing on a full year of unit-level, PLC-level, student-reflection, and parent-reporting artifacts
- milestones-3-target_date: 2026-07-24
- milestones-3-improvement_type: compliance-accountability
- criteria-0-text: Each of the seven Schoolwide Learner Outcomes — Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, Confident, Engaged, Innovative, Reflective, and Responsible — carries a published school-authored behavioral descriptor at the depth of the current Confident descriptor, authored collaboratively by faculty across the bilingual program, subject departments, homeroom system, and co-curricular program and validated by divisional leadership, and the descriptor set is posted in the staff handbook, the student-facing learning materials, and the parent reporting portal.
- criteria-0-verification_kind: inspection
- criteria-1-text: Every department's unit plans for the 2025-2026 cycle name the one or two SLOs each unit advances and attach at least one assessment artifact per unit that produces SLO-tagged evidence of student progress, verifiable by inspecting the unit-plan repository for the year.
- criteria-1-verification_kind: target
- criteria-1-target_value: 100
- criteria-1-target_unit: percent of departmental units in the 2025-2026 cycle whose unit plan names its advancing SLO(s) and links at least one SLO-tagged assessment artifact
- criteria-1-baseline: 0
- criteria-2-text: PLC cycles in every division open with an SLO frame on the agenda and close with SLO-tagged evidence of student learning recorded in the PLC minutes, evidenced by a leadership review of PLC minutes across the year.
- criteria-2-verification_kind: target
- criteria-2-target_value: 90
- criteria-2-target_unit: percent of logged PLC cycles across all divisions in the 2025-2026 cycle whose minutes show both an opening SLO frame and a closing SLO-tagged evidence entry
- criteria-2-baseline: 0
- criteria-3-text: Parent reporting at each reporting cycle of 2025-2026 includes an SLO-organized narrative section, in addition to subject grades, for every enrolled student, with the SLO narrative section present in the report template and populated for each student on each cycle.
- criteria-3-verification_kind: inspection
- criteria-4-text: Students use the SLO vocabulary in self-reflection at term reporting and at portfolio review and can name which SLOs a given piece of their work demonstrates, as judged by divisional leadership against a shared rubric applied to a sampled set of student reflections and portfolio entries from each division.
- criteria-4-verification_kind: judgment
- criteria-5-text: The annual self-study against WASC standards A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1 is organized with the seven SLOs as the categories under which evidence is gathered and presented, with each of the six standards' evidence section structured by SLO and drawing on SLO-tagged classroom walkthrough notes, work-sample reviews, PLC minutes, and parent-reporting narratives produced during the cycle.
- criteria-5-verification_kind: inspection
- learner_outcomes_targeted-0-label: Bilingual Communicators
- learner_outcomes_targeted-0-rationale: The plan explicitly names Bilingual Communicators as the SLO addressed by the Bilingual Environment area-for-improvement work, and the desired state requires shared behavioral descriptors and SLO-tagged assessment evidence across the bilingual program.
- learner_outcomes_targeted-1-label: Compassionate
- learner_outcomes_targeted-1-rationale: The rationale names Compassionate as one of the lived-norm outcomes that School Culture work will operationalize, and the desired state requires a school-authored behavioral descriptor for this SLO at the depth of the current Confident descriptor.
- learner_outcomes_targeted-2-label: Confident: Shows self-assurance in learning and communicating, knowing that growth happens through taking risks and expressing their ideas.
- learner_outcomes_targeted-2-rationale: Confident is the one SLO with an existing published descriptor and is the depth-benchmark the plan uses for authoring the other six descriptors; it is therefore directly in scope as the model the school will validate and extend.
- learner_outcomes_targeted-3-label: Engaged
- learner_outcomes_targeted-3-rationale: Engaged is named in the rationale as a lived-norm outcome for School Culture work and is one of the six single-word labels the desired state commits to giving a shared behavioral descriptor, unit-plan naming, and SLO-tagged assessment evidence.
- learner_outcomes_targeted-4-label: Innovative
- learner_outcomes_targeted-4-rationale: Innovative is among the six SLOs currently functioning as a single-word label without a shared descriptor; the desired state requires it to carry a faculty-authored descriptor, appear in unit plans, and produce SLO-tagged assessment evidence like every other outcome.
- learner_outcomes_targeted-5-label: Reflective
- learner_outcomes_targeted-5-rationale: Reflective is directly implicated by the desired-state commitment that students use SLO vocabulary in self-reflection at term reporting and portfolio review and can name which SLOs a piece of their work demonstrates.
- learner_outcomes_targeted-6-label: Responsible
- learner_outcomes_targeted-6-rationale: Responsible is named in the rationale as a lived-norm outcome for School Culture work and is one of the six labels the desired state commits to giving a shared behavioral descriptor and SLO-tagged classroom evidence.
- mission_areas_targeted-0-label: Academic excellence
- mission_areas_targeted-0-rationale: The rationale states the SLOs are the operational expression of Academic excellence, and the desired state ties SLO-tagged unit design, assessment artifacts, and PLC evidence cycles directly to instructional quality across every department.
- mission_areas_targeted-1-label: Caring and safe community
- mission_areas_targeted-1-rationale: The rationale identifies the SLOs as the operational expression of Caring and safe community, and the desired state makes Compassionate, Responsible, and Engaged lived norms through School Culture work and shared stakeholder vocabulary.
- mission_areas_targeted-2-label: Global citizenship
- mission_areas_targeted-2-rationale: The rationale explicitly names Global citizenship as one of the three mission areas the SLOs operationally express, and outcomes like Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, and Responsible are the school's daily-practice expression of that mission area.
- areas_for_improvement-0-label: Bilingual Environment
- areas_for_improvement-0-rationale: The rationale ties the Bilingual Communicators SLO directly to Bilingual Environment work, and the desired state extends SLO naming and tagging across the bilingual program's unit plans and assessments.
- areas_for_improvement-1-label: Curriculum and Learning
- areas_for_improvement-1-rationale: The desired state requires every department's unit plans to name the SLOs each unit advances and to produce at least one SLO-tagged assessment artifact per unit — the core of Curriculum and Learning.
- areas_for_improvement-2-label: Faculty PD and PLC
- areas_for_improvement-2-rationale: The plan requires faculty to collaboratively author behavioral descriptors for six SLOs and to open and close every PLC cycle with SLO framing and SLO-tagged evidence — descriptor authoring and PLC redesign are Faculty PD and PLC work.
- areas_for_improvement-3-label: School Culture
- areas_for_improvement-3-rationale: The rationale identifies Compassionate, Responsible, and Engaged as lived norms to be operationalized through School Culture work, moving SLOs from wall posters to shared community language.
- areas_for_improvement-4-label: Student Data and Well-being
- areas_for_improvement-4-rationale: The current state notes student-data conversations rarely cite an SLO as the interpretive lens; the desired state requires SLO-tagged evidence in PLC data cycles and term-level student self-reflection, both Student Data and Well-being concerns.
- areas_for_improvement-5-label: Community Involvement
- areas_for_improvement-5-rationale: The desired state requires SLO-organized narrative sections in parent reporting at each reporting cycle, and the rationale names Community Involvement as the domain through which parent-facing SLO communication is delivered.
- areas_for_improvement-6-label: Resources and Tools
- areas_for_improvement-6-rationale: The rationale names Resources and Tools as the domain that supplies the templates and gradebook fields that carry SLO tagging — the infrastructure the unit-plan naming, assessment tagging, and parent reporting all depend on.
- stakeholder_impact-0-label: Administration / Leadership
- stakeholder_impact-0-rationale: The desired state requires leadership to use SLOs as the organizing categories for classroom walkthroughs, work-sample reviews, and the annual WASC self-study, and the rationale names Administration / Leadership as a stakeholder that must share the SLO vocabulary under standard C1.
- stakeholder_impact-1-label: Staff / Faculty
- stakeholder_impact-1-rationale: Faculty author the behavioral descriptors, name SLOs in unit plans, generate SLO-tagged assessment artifacts, and open and close every PLC cycle with an SLO frame — the plan's central operational load sits with Staff / Faculty.
- stakeholder_impact-2-label: Students
- stakeholder_impact-2-rationale: The desired state requires students to use SLO vocabulary in self-reflection at term reporting and portfolio review and to name which SLOs a given piece of their work demonstrates, making Students direct users of the new instrument.
- stakeholder_impact-3-label: Parents
- stakeholder_impact-3-rationale: Parent reporting at each reporting cycle is to include an SLO-organized narrative section in addition to subject grades, and the rationale names Parents as a stakeholder group that must share the SLO vocabulary under standard C1.
- stakeholder_impact-4-label: Parent Committee
- stakeholder_impact-4-rationale: The rationale explicitly names the Parent Committee among the stakeholder groups that must share a common SLO vocabulary for what growth means, situating the committee as a partner in Community Involvement and SLO-organized parent reporting.


Improvement types in play at this school:

The school's planning-method recipes (CHOOSE EXACTLY ONE by its name; do not invent a method):
- "Data quality improvement" — Improves the visibility and usability of a data feed. (applies to: data-collection-improvement)
  Ordered step templates:
    1. Define metrics and intended decisions
    2. Revise instrument / collection method
    3. Collect baseline
    4. Publish dashboard / report to consumers
    5. Review usability with consumers; iterate
- "Event design (recurring)" — Design and run a school event with debrief cycle. (applies to: event-design)
  Ordered step templates:
    1. Draft schedule
    2. Assign personnel
    3. Prepare materials
    4. Run event
    5. Debrief; revise for next cycle
- "Policy revision (6-step consultative)" — Surfaces the propose→consult→revise→publish→propagate cycle. Used for the 2025-26 SLO revision. (applies to: policy-establishment, policy-revision)
  Ordered step templates:
    1. Present options at admin meeting
    2. Finalize initial draft
    3. Publicize draft to stakeholders for feedback
    4. Integrate feedback
    5. Finalize after feedback and publish
    6. Propagate / embed into downstream artifacts
- "Tool / platform customization" — Customize a platform for school needs, then train and support. (applies to: tool-or-platform)
  Ordered step templates:
    1. Customize platform for school needs
    2. Build training materials and run training
    3. Provide ongoing support
    4. Collect user feedback; iterate

Pick the single method whose ordered step templates best fit this plan's draft and improvement types. Expand THAT method's templates, in order, into concrete action steps written for this specific plan. Produce one step per template in the chosen method (preserve the template order). Write each step's "description" as the concrete work to do; write each step's "assessment" as how completion of that step will be checked (an empty string is acceptable when no assessment is warranted). Choose ONLY from the provided methods; do not invent methodology.

Every actor, unit, role, position-title, or owning body named in `description` or `assessment` MUST be a verbatim match to a Division or Position label from the divisions catalogue above; every stakeholder named MUST be a label from the stakeholder_groups catalogue; every policy named MUST be a label from the policies catalogue. The school's organizational roster is exactly what the catalogues enumerate; do not name roles, departments, or offices not present.

OUTPUT CONTRACT (follow exactly):
Return ONE JSON object and nothing else. Do not wrap it in markdown code fences. Do not write any prose before or after the JSON. Write all values in English only.

The object MUST have EXACTLY these two keys:
- "method": the exact name of the single chosen method, copied verbatim from one of the provided method names above.
- "steps": a non-empty JSON array, one element per the chosen method's step templates, in template order. Each element is an object with EXACTLY two string keys:
  - "description": the concrete action step for this plan.
  - "assessment": how completion of this step is checked (may be an empty string).

No nested objects beyond this shape, no extra keys, no markdown.

planning_methods.name: Data quality improvement

planning_methods.rationale: Design and run a school event with debrief cycle.

planning_methods.name: Policy revision (6-step consultative)
planning_methods.rationale: Surfaces the propose→consult→revise→publish→propagate cycle. Used for the 2025-26 SLO revision.
steps: Publicize draft to stakeholders for feedback

planning_methods.name: Tool / platform customization
planning_methods.rationale: Customize a platform for school needs, then train and support.
steps: Build training materials and run training

draft_state.current_state: Chiway-Repton School Xiamen has adopted seven Schoolwide Learner Outcomes — Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, Confident, Engaged, Innovative, Reflective, and Responsible — but they currently function more as a published list than as an operational instrument. The SLOs appear in orientation materials and on classroom walls, yet they are not consistently named in unit planning, not consistently surfaced in formative or summative assessment design, not consistently invoked in student-facing feedback or self-reflection routines, and not consistently used by divisional leadership when reviewing teaching practice or student work. The Confident outcome has the only published behavioral descriptor; the other six are single-word labels without shared classroom-level definitions, which means teachers across the bilingual program, the subject departments, the homeroom system, and the co-curricular program apply them inconsistently or not at all. Student data conversations, PLC agendas, and parent communications rarely cite an SLO as the lens through which a result is being interpreted, so the link from mission to daily practice is weak in evidence even where it is strong in intent.
draft_state.desired_state: By the end of the 2025-2026 accreditation cycle, the seven SLOs are the school's working language for learning across all four framing audiences — schoolwide, teaching, student, and parent. Each SLO carries a shared, school-authored behavioral descriptor at the depth of the current Confident descriptor, written collaboratively by faculty and validated by leadership. Every department's unit plans name the one or two SLOs each unit advances, with at least one assessment artifact per unit that produces SLO-tagged evidence of student progress. Students use the SLO vocabulary in self-reflection at defined points in the year — minimally at term reporting and at portfolio review — and can name which SLOs a given piece of their work demonstrates. PLC cycles in every division open with an SLO frame and close with SLO-tagged evidence of student learning. Parent reporting at each reporting cycle includes an SLO-organized narrative section in addition to subject grades. Leadership reviews — classroom walkthroughs, work-sample reviews, and the annual self-study against WASC standards A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1 — use the SLOs as the organizing categories for what is being looked for and what counts as evidence.
draft_state.rationale: WASC standard A1 requires that the school's mission be collectively understood and actively implemented across the school community, and the SLOs are the operational expression of Chiway-Repton's mission areas of Academic excellence, Caring and safe community, and Global citizenship; an SLO set that is published but not practiced leaves A1 underevidenced. Standard A4 requires data-driven continuous improvement, and without SLO-tagged evidence the school cannot show longitudinal progress against its own stated learner profile. Standards B1, B2, and B3 require curriculum, instruction, and assessment that respond to student needs and measure progress against shared outcomes — the SLOs are precisely those shared outcomes, and at present they do not consistently shape unit design, classroom practice, or assessment analysis. Standard C1 requires shared responsibility for student growth, which is hard to demonstrate when stakeholder groups — Administration / Leadership, Staff / Faculty, Students, Parents, and the Parent Committee — are not using a common vocabulary for what growth means. This work is timed for the 2025-2026 cycle because the school is entering the self-study window and because seven of the eight area-for-improvement domains touch SLO usage directly: Bilingual Environment (the Bilingual Communicators outcome), Curriculum and Learning (SLO-aligned unit and assessment design), Faculty PD and PLC (descriptor authoring and SLO-framed PLC cycles), School Culture (Compassionate, Responsible, and Engaged as lived norms), Student Data and Well-being (SLO-tagged evidence in data conversations), Community Involvement (SLO-organized parent reporting), and Resources and Tools (the templates and gradebook fields that carry SLO tagging). Acting now positions the school to enter the self-study with a year of operating evidence rather than a retrospective claim.
draft_state.student_impact_framing: Students experience a school in which the seven learner outcomes are not posters but the actual language adults and peers use to describe learning. A student can name which SLOs a unit is advancing before the unit begins, can point to artifacts in their own work that demonstrate progress on specific SLOs, and can speak to their growth on each SLO in term reflections and portfolio reviews. Bilingual Communicators becomes legible in concrete language-use evidence across both Chinese-medium and English-medium settings rather than an aspiration tied only to language-class grades. Confident, Reflective, and Engaged become observable habits the student is supported to develop, with feedback that names the SLO it is strengthening. Compassionate and Responsible become operative norms in the homeroom and co-curricular program, recognized in real situations rather than awarded generically. Innovative becomes visible in tasks that explicitly invite it and in evidence the student themselves curates. Parents receive reporting that lets them see their child's growth as a learner along the school's stated outcomes, not only as a subject-grade aggregate, which gives families a shared vocabulary with the school for supporting growth at home.
draft_state.provenance: This plan draws on the school's existing published SLO set (Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, Confident, Engaged, Innovative, Reflective, Responsible), the existing behavioral descriptor for Confident as the model for the descriptor-authoring work, the school's stated mission areas (Academic excellence, Caring and safe community, Global citizenship), and the seven area-for-improvement domains the school has identified as priorities for this cycle. It is anchored to the WASC 2025-2026 accreditation standards, specifically A1 (Vision and Mission), A4 (Continuous Improvement), B1 (Curriculum), B2 (Teaching and Learning), B3 (Assessment and Data Analysis), and C1 (School Culture). It assumes the stakeholder groups already named in the school's plan structure — Administration / Leadership, Staff / Faculty, Students, Parents, Parent Committee, and Admissions Applicants — as the audiences for the SLO vocabulary and reporting. Operating evidence to be generated during the cycle includes the authored SLO descriptors, unit plans with SLO tagging, SLO-tagged assessment artifacts, PLC minutes opening and closing on SLO frames, classroom walkthrough notes organized by SLO, term and portfolio student reflections, and the SLO-organized section of parent reporting; these constitute the evidence base the self-study will cite.
draft_state.milestones-0-label: Seven SLO behavioral descriptors authored by cross-divisional faculty teams and validated by the Senior Leadership Team, each at the depth of the existing Confident descriptor and published in the staff handbook, parent handbook, and student-facing materials
draft_state.milestones-1-label: Unit-planning template and gradebook updated so every department's Term 2 unit plans name one or two SLOs per unit and carry at least one SLO-tagged assessment artifact, with PLC cycles in every division opening on an SLO frame and closing on SLO-tagged evidence
draft_state.milestones-2-label: Term reporting and portfolio-review routines deliver an SLO-organized parent narrative for every enrolled student and an SLO self-reflection from every student, across EY, Primary, Secondary, and the bilingual program
draft_state.milestones-3-label: Annual self-study evidence binder against WASC A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1 compiled with the seven SLOs as the organizing evidence categories, drawing on a full year of unit-level, PLC-level, student-reflection, and parent-reporting artifacts
draft_state.criteria-0-text: Each of the seven Schoolwide Learner Outcomes — Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, Confident, Engaged, Innovative, Reflective, and Responsible — carries a published school-authored behavioral descriptor at the depth of the current Confident descriptor, authored collaboratively by faculty across the bilingual program, subject departments, homeroom system, and co-curricular program and validated by divisional leadership, and the descriptor set is posted in the staff handbook, the student-facing learning materials, and the parent reporting portal.
draft_state.criteria-1-text: Every department's unit plans for the 2025-2026 cycle name the one or two SLOs each unit advances and attach at least one assessment artifact per unit that produces SLO-tagged evidence of student progress, verifiable by inspecting the unit-plan repository for the year.
draft_state.criteria-1-target_unit: percent of departmental units in the 2025-2026 cycle whose unit plan names its advancing SLO(s) and links at least one SLO-tagged assessment artifact
draft_state.criteria-2-text: PLC cycles in every division open with an SLO frame on the agenda and close with SLO-tagged evidence of student learning recorded in the PLC minutes, evidenced by a leadership review of PLC minutes across the year.
draft_state.criteria-2-target_unit: percent of logged PLC cycles across all divisions in the 2025-2026 cycle whose minutes show both an opening SLO frame and a closing SLO-tagged evidence entry
draft_state.criteria-3-text: Parent reporting at each reporting cycle of 2025-2026 includes an SLO-organized narrative section, in addition to subject grades, for every enrolled student, with the SLO narrative section present in the report template and populated for each student on each cycle.
draft_state.criteria-4-text: Students use the SLO vocabulary in self-reflection at term reporting and at portfolio review and can name which SLOs a given piece of their work demonstrates, as judged by divisional leadership against a shared rubric applied to a sampled set of student reflections and portfolio entries from each division.
draft_state.criteria-5-text: The annual self-study against WASC standards A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1 is organized with the seven SLOs as the categories under which evidence is gathered and presented, with each of the six standards' evidence section structured by SLO and drawing on SLO-tagged classroom walkthrough notes, work-sample reviews, PLC minutes, and parent-reporting narratives produced during the cycle.
draft_state.learner_outcomes_targeted-0-rationale: The plan explicitly names Bilingual Communicators as the SLO addressed by the Bilingual Environment area-for-improvement work, and the desired state requires shared behavioral descriptors and SLO-tagged assessment evidence across the bilingual program.
draft_state.learner_outcomes_targeted-1-rationale: The rationale names Compassionate as one of the lived-norm outcomes that School Culture work will operationalize, and the desired state requires a school-authored behavioral descriptor for this SLO at the depth of the current Confident descriptor.
draft_state.learner_outcomes_targeted-2-label: Confident: Shows self-assurance in learning and communicating, knowing that growth happens through taking risks and expressing their ideas.
draft_state.learner_outcomes_targeted-2-rationale: Confident is the one SLO with an existing published descriptor and is the depth-benchmark the plan uses for authoring the other six descriptors; it is therefore directly in scope as the model the school will validate and extend.
draft_state.learner_outcomes_targeted-3-rationale: Engaged is named in the rationale as a lived-norm outcome for School Culture work and is one of the six single-word labels the desired state commits to giving a shared behavioral descriptor, unit-plan naming, and SLO-tagged assessment evidence.
draft_state.learner_outcomes_targeted-4-rationale: Innovative is among the six SLOs currently functioning as a single-word label without a shared descriptor; the desired state requires it to carry a faculty-authored descriptor, appear in unit plans, and produce SLO-tagged assessment evidence like every other outcome.
draft_state.learner_outcomes_targeted-5-rationale: Reflective is directly implicated by the desired-state commitment that students use SLO vocabulary in self-reflection at term reporting and portfolio review and can name which SLOs a piece of their work demonstrates.
draft_state.learner_outcomes_targeted-6-rationale: Responsible is named in the rationale as a lived-norm outcome for School Culture work and is one of the six labels the desired state commits to giving a shared behavioral descriptor and SLO-tagged classroom evidence.
draft_state.mission_areas_targeted-0-rationale: The rationale states the SLOs are the operational expression of Academic excellence, and the desired state ties SLO-tagged unit design, assessment artifacts, and PLC evidence cycles directly to instructional quality across every department.
draft_state.mission_areas_targeted-1-rationale: The rationale identifies the SLOs as the operational expression of Caring and safe community, and the desired state makes Compassionate, Responsible, and Engaged lived norms through School Culture work and shared stakeholder vocabulary.
draft_state.mission_areas_targeted-2-rationale: The rationale explicitly names Global citizenship as one of the three mission areas the SLOs operationally express, and outcomes like Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, and Responsible are the school's daily-practice expression of that mission area.
draft_state.areas_for_improvement-0-rationale: The rationale ties the Bilingual Communicators SLO directly to Bilingual Environment work, and the desired state extends SLO naming and tagging across the bilingual program's unit plans and assessments.
draft_state.areas_for_improvement-1-rationale: The desired state requires every department's unit plans to name the SLOs each unit advances and to produce at least one SLO-tagged assessment artifact per unit — the core of Curriculum and Learning.
draft_state.areas_for_improvement-2-rationale: The plan requires faculty to collaboratively author behavioral descriptors for six SLOs and to open and close every PLC cycle with SLO framing and SLO-tagged evidence — descriptor authoring and PLC redesign are Faculty PD and PLC work.
draft_state.areas_for_improvement-3-rationale: The rationale identifies Compassionate, Responsible, and Engaged as lived norms to be operationalized through School Culture work, moving SLOs from wall posters to shared community language.
draft_state.areas_for_improvement-4-rationale: The current state notes student-data conversations rarely cite an SLO as the interpretive lens; the desired state requires SLO-tagged evidence in PLC data cycles and term-level student self-reflection, both Student Data and Well-being concerns.
draft_state.areas_for_improvement-5-rationale: The desired state requires SLO-organized narrative sections in parent reporting at each reporting cycle, and the rationale names Community Involvement as the domain through which parent-facing SLO communication is delivered.
draft_state.areas_for_improvement-6-rationale: The rationale names Resources and Tools as the domain that supplies the templates and gradebook fields that carry SLO tagging — the infrastructure the unit-plan naming, assessment tagging, and parent reporting all depend on.
draft_state.stakeholder_impact-0-rationale: The desired state requires leadership to use SLOs as the organizing categories for classroom walkthroughs, work-sample reviews, and the annual WASC self-study, and the rationale names Administration / Leadership as a stakeholder that must share the SLO vocabulary under standard C1.
draft_state.stakeholder_impact-1-rationale: Faculty author the behavioral descriptors, name SLOs in unit plans, generate SLO-tagged assessment artifacts, and open and close every PLC cycle with an SLO frame — the plan's central operational load sits with Staff / Faculty.
draft_state.stakeholder_impact-2-rationale: The desired state requires students to use SLO vocabulary in self-reflection at term reporting and portfolio review and to name which SLOs a given piece of their work demonstrates, making Students direct users of the new instrument.
draft_state.stakeholder_impact-3-rationale: Parent reporting at each reporting cycle is to include an SLO-organized narrative section in addition to subject grades, and the rationale names Parents as a stakeholder group that must share the SLO vocabulary under standard C1.
draft_state.stakeholder_impact-4-rationale: The rationale explicitly names the Parent Committee among the stakeholder groups that must share a common SLO vocabulary for what growth means, situating the committee as a partner in Community Involvement and SLO-organized parent reporting.

agent_response_parsed.description: The Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center presents options at an admin meeting convened with the IHS Principal, the Director of AAO, the Director of Student Affairs Office, and the Director of Future Scholar Innovation Center for authoring shared behavioral descriptors for the six undefined Schoolwide Learner Outcomes (Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, Engaged, Innovative, Reflective, Responsible) at the depth of the existing Confident descriptor. Options cover descriptor-authoring sequencing, cross-divisional faculty team composition drawing on Heads of English, Humanities, Math, Sciences, Art Department, Business and Economics, Music and Sports Department, and the College Counseling Department Coordinator, validation pathway through divisional leaders, and the downstream embedding scope into unit-plan templates, PLC agendas, term-reporting templates, portfolio-review rubrics, classroom walkthrough instruments, and the WASC self-study evidence binder organized against standards A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1.
agent_response_parsed.assessment: Admin meeting minutes recorded by the IHS Assistant show the options were presented and a single option was selected for advancement; the selected option is on file in the Principal's Office (PO).

agent_response_parsed.description: The Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center, supported by the Senior Teaching Coordinator and the PD Leader, finalizes an initial draft of the seven SLO behavioral descriptor set. The draft is authored by cross-divisional faculty teams pairing the Head of English, Head of Humanities, Head of Math, Head of Sciences, Head of Art Department, Head of Business and Economics, and a Music Teacher and PE Teacher from the Music and Sports Department, with each team writing one descriptor for an unwritten SLO using the existing Confident descriptor as the depth model; the Homeroom Teacher cohort under the Director of Student Affairs Office contributes pastoral framing for Compassionate, Responsible, and Engaged; the College Counseling Department Coordinator contributes outcome framing tied to onward pathways; the Librarian contributes language tied to information-literacy expression of Reflective and Innovative; and the Director of Future Scholar Innovation Center contributes framing for Innovative. The draft also includes companion language for the unit-plan template, the PLC agenda frame, the term-reporting SLO narrative section, and the portfolio-review reflection prompts.
agent_response_parsed.assessment: A single initial draft document containing the seven descriptors and the companion language is held by the Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center and circulated to the IHS Principal for the consultation stage.

agent_response_parsed.description: The IHS Principal and the Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center publicize the initial draft to stakeholders for feedback. Staff / Faculty receive the draft through division-led review sessions run by the Head of English, Head of Humanities, Head of Math, Head of Sciences, Head of Art Department, Head of Business and Economics, and the Music and Sports Department, with the AAO - Academic Affairs Office circulating the draft to all teaching staff via the Academic Affairs Officer. Students receive the draft through Homeroom Teacher sessions coordinated by the A2 Year Leader, AS Year Leader, IG1 Year Leader, and IG2 Year Leader under the Director of Student Affairs Office, with response gathered in homeroom. Parents and the Parent Committee receive the draft through a written communication issued from the Principal's Office (PO) and a feedback session convened by the IHS Principal. Administration / Leadership review the draft through the existing leadership meeting cadence chaired by the IHS Principal. The College Counselor circulates the draft to current Admissions Applicants families through the College Counseling Department for forward-looking visibility.
agent_response_parsed.assessment: Feedback intake log maintained by the IHS Assistant records receipt of written and meeting-captured feedback from Staff / Faculty (by division), Students (by year), Parents, the Parent Committee, and Administration / Leadership, with date and channel for each entry.

agent_response_parsed.description: The Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center, with the Senior Teaching Coordinator and the PD Leader, integrates the feedback into a revised descriptor set and the companion language for unit-plan templates, PLC agenda frames, term-reporting SLO narrative sections, and portfolio-review reflection prompts. Each Head of Department (Head of English, Head of Humanities, Head of Math, Head of Sciences, Head of Art Department, Head of Business and Economics) signs off on the revisions affecting their department's unit-plan implications; the Director of Student Affairs Office signs off on revisions to descriptors tied to Compassionate, Responsible, and Engaged and to homeroom-facing student reflection language; the Director of AAO signs off on the gradebook-field and reporting-template language to be carried by the AAO - Academic Affairs Office; the Director of Future Scholar Innovation Center signs off on Innovative; and the College Counseling Department Coordinator signs off on outcome framing tied to onward pathways.
agent_response_parsed.assessment: Revision tracking document maintained by the Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center shows each feedback item received in step 3 and its disposition (incorporated, modified, or declined with stated reason), with departmental and divisional sign-offs recorded against the revised draft.

agent_response_parsed.description: The IHS Principal finalizes the revised SLO descriptor set after feedback and publishes it. Publication channels include the staff handbook (maintained by the Principal's Office (PO) with distribution support from the IHS Assistant), the student-facing learning materials (distributed by Homeroom Teachers across all year groups under the Director of Student Affairs Office), and the parent reporting portal and parent handbook (issued by the Principal's Office (PO)). The AAO - Academic Affairs Office, under the Director of AAO, publishes the descriptors into the unit-plan template repository and the gradebook reference fields. The Librarian deposits a reference copy in the Library collection.
agent_response_parsed.assessment: Published copies of the seven SLO behavioral descriptors are present in the staff handbook, the parent handbook, the parent reporting portal, the unit-plan template repository, the gradebook reference, and the Library collection, verified by the IHS Assistant against a publication checklist held in the Principal's Office (PO).

agent_response_parsed.description: The Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center propagates and embeds the published descriptors into downstream artifacts so the descriptors operate as the school's working language for learning. The unit-plan template carried by the AAO - Academic Affairs Office requires every department's units for the 2025-2026 cycle to name the one or two SLOs each unit advances and to attach at least one SLO-tagged assessment artifact, with the Senior Teaching Coordinator and the PD Leader supporting each Head of Department (Head of English, Head of Humanities, Head of Math, Head of Sciences, Head of Art Department, Head of Business and Economics) in embedding the descriptors into their department's unit plans. PLC cycles in every division open with an SLO frame and close with SLO-tagged evidence in PLC minutes, coordinated by the PD Leader. Term reporting and portfolio review, administered through the AAO - Academic Affairs Office and Homeroom Teachers under the Director of Student Affairs Office across all year groups led by the A2 Year Leader, AS Year Leader, IG1 Year Leader, and IG2 Year Leader, carry an SLO-organized parent narrative and an SLO student self-reflection. Classroom walkthroughs and work-sample reviews conducted by the IHS Principal, the Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center, and each Head of Department are organized by SLO. The annual self-study against WASC standards A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1 is compiled by the Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center with the IHS Principal, using the seven SLOs as the organizing evidence categories and drawing on SLO-tagged unit plans, PLC minutes, walkthrough notes, work-sample reviews, student reflections, and parent reports produced during the cycle. The Class Schedule Officer in the AAO - Academic Affairs Office ensures PLC time is held in the schedule. The Director of Future Scholar Innovation Center embeds the Innovative descriptor into Future Scholar Innovation Center programs, and the College Counseling Department uses the descriptor set in counseling conversations with students.
agent_response_parsed.assessment: End-of-cycle inspection by the IHS Principal and the Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center confirms: 100 percent of departmental units in the 2025-2026 cycle have unit plans naming advancing SLO(s) and linking at least one SLO-tagged assessment artifact (verified against the unit-plan repository held by the AAO - Academic Affairs Office); at least 90 percent of logged PLC cycles across all divisions show both opening SLO frame and closing SLO-tagged evidence in their minutes; the SLO narrative section is present and populated in parent reports for every enrolled student at each reporting cycle; student SLO self-reflections are on file from term reporting and portfolio review for every enrolled student; and the WASC self-study evidence binder for standards A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1 is organized by the seven SLOs with citations to the cycle's artifacts.
